svg.title: BSE Data Structure — Column Analysis & Join Keys
svg.desc: Shows key columns from BSE Client Master and BSE SIP file, their join keys, and what each provides
svg.text: Primary Holder PAN → join to CAMS folio
KYC Type, CKYC Number, Aadhaar flag
Account Type / Account No / IFSC Code
MICR No, Bank Name, Bank Branch
Mandate linked via Mandate ID in SIP file
Email → secondary join to CAMS/KFin AUM
XSIP Regn No → unique SIP identifier
RTA Scheme Code → join to CAMS/KFin
Folio No → join to CAMS folio master
Mandate ID → join to client bank details
Brokerage (always 0 in BSE — use RTA file)

g.text: Folio No in SIP file — present (48914251). Direct join to CAMS folio master. No PAN lookup needed as primary path.
RTA Scheme Code — present (689G). Can join to CAMS scheme tables directly. No AMFI lookup needed for basic join.
Brokerage column in SIP = always 0 — never use this. Real brokerage comes from CAMS/KFin RTA files only.
Client Code = UCC — "DISHIK" is alphanumeric, not a number. Current schema stores as TEXT — correct.
First Order flag — in SIP file (N here). Maps directly to existing first_order column in holdings table.
Client Master has 5 bank accounts — current schema stores nothing from client master except name/PAN/email/mobile.
Nominee data (3 nominees) — completely ignored in current schema. Entire nominee section is dropped on import.
No. Of Installments — present in SIP file. Not stored currently. Useful for SIP end-date calculation + progress tracking.
XSIP Regn No — unique SIP ID (184325919). Should be stored as dedup key, replaces current folio+scheme+date combo.
SIP file has client email + mobile — redundant with client master but useful for quick SIP-level contact lookup.
